title: Server Configuration, HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2, TLS, and Graceful Shutdown · Mastery Check
summary: A production-readiness review compares near-miss decisions for server configuration, http/1.1, http/2, tls, and graceful shutdown and requires the smallest safe explicit http.Server protocol and shutdown configuration with changed-request evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for explicit http.Server protocol and shutdown configuration. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Configure ReadHeaderTimeout, body strategy, WriteTimeout, IdleTimeout, MaxHeaderBytes, and per-handler deadlines from threat and workload evidence.
Configure Server.Protocols for HTTP/1 and TLS HTTP/2, treat UnencryptedHTTP2 as explicit h2c authority, and name HTTP/3 as outside net/http server support.
Set HTTP2Config stream, frame, compression-table, flow-control, ping, write-byte, and error-count boundaries without confusing transport and server fields.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Shutdown Close Register
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz team handles case 8115 in a partner API with an outdated generated client. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate HTTP/1.1 and HTTP/2 service by timeout and graceful-drain policy while excluding HTTP/3; then repeat the request with a different method and retain status plus durable-effect evidence. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use Shutdown deadlines, readiness withdrawal, listener stop, in-flight drain, hijacked-connection handling, RegisterOnShutdown, Close fallback, and exit evidence.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: Go server Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Shutdown Close Register.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use shutdown deadlines, readiness withdrawal, listener stop, in-flight drain, hijacked-connection handling, registeronshutdown, close fallback, and exit evidence. For Go server Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Shutdown Close Register, keep Shutdown Close Register connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.
